questions.q: Who demanded the two boons from King Dasharatha?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi, the youngest queen of Dasharatha, demanded the two boons she had been promised.

questions.q: From which king did Kaikeyi claim her two boons?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi reminded her husband King Dasharatha of the two boons he had earlier granted her.

questions.q: How many boons did Kaikeyi demand from Dasharatha?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi invoked two boons that Dasharatha had once promised her.

questions.q: Through her first boon, whom did Kaikeyi want crowned king?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi's first boon demanded that her own son Bharata be installed as king.

questions.q: Through her second boon, whom did Kaikeyi want sent into exile?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi's second boon demanded that Rama be banished to the forest.

questions.q: For how many years did Kaikeyi demand that Rama be exiled?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi demanded fourteen years of forest exile for Rama.

questions.q: To where was Rama to be sent according to Kaikeyi's demand?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi demanded that Rama live in the forest in exile for fourteen years.

questions.q: Which maidservant turned Kaikeyi's mind against Rama?
questions.explanation: Manthara, Kaikeyi's hunchbacked maid, poisoned the queen's mind with jealous counsel.

questions.q: What event was Dasharatha planning when Kaikeyi made her demand?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha had announced Rama's coronation as the heir apparent, which Kaikeyi's boons overturned.

questions.q: Who was Kaikeyi's son who was to be made king?
questions.explanation: Bharata, born to Kaikeyi, was the son she wished to place on the throne of Ayodhya.

questions.q: In which war had Kaikeyi originally earned the two boons?
options: A war between the gods and asuras (devasura war)
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi accompanied Dasharatha in a devasura battle and earned the boons by saving his life.

questions.q: How did Kaikeyi save Dasharatha's life, earning the boons?
options: She healed his wounds with herbs
She tended his chariot when its axle pin failed
She prayed to the gods for him
questions.explanation: In the battle, Kaikeyi steadied the chariot's broken wheel, and the grateful Dasharatha granted her two boons.

questions.q: Where did Kaikeyi go to await Dasharatha and press her demand?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi retired in fury to the 'kopa-bhavana,' the anger chamber, lying on the bare floor.

questions.q: How did Dasharatha react when Kaikeyi stated her two demands?
options: He fainted in grief and despair
questions.explanation: Dasharatha was overcome with anguish, swooning and pleading, for he could not bear to part with Rama.

questions.q: Why did Dasharatha ultimately yield to Kaikeyi's demands?
options: To keep his word and uphold truth (satya)
questions.explanation: Bound by his promise, Dasharatha honored truth and his given word even at the cost of his beloved son.

questions.q: How did Rama respond when told of Kaikeyi's demand for his exile?
options: He calmly accepted to honor his father's word
questions.explanation: Rama accepted the exile serenely, resolved to uphold his father's promise and dharma.

questions.q: Who was the chief queen and Rama's mother, distressed by the boons?
questions.explanation: Kaushalya, the senior queen and Rama's mother, grieved deeply at his banishment.

questions.q: Where was Bharata when Kaikeyi made her demands?
options: Away at his maternal grandfather's kingdom
questions.explanation: Bharata was visiting his maternal uncle's home (Kekaya) and was absent from Ayodhya during these events.

questions.q: In which book (kanda) of the Ramayana does the episode of Kaikeyi's boons occur?
questions.explanation: The demand for the boons and Rama's exile is narrated in the Ayodhya Kanda.

questions.q: How did Lakshmana initially react to the news of Rama's exile?
options: With fury, ready to oppose it by force
questions.explanation: Lakshmana was enraged and wished to resist, but Rama calmed him and chose to obey their father.

questions.q: What did Kaikeyi demand Rama wear as he departed for the forest?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi insisted Rama don bark cloth and the garb of a forest ascetic for his exile.

questions.q: Who accompanied Rama into exile along with Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: Sita, Rama's wife, insisted on following him into the forest, as did his brother Lakshmana.

questions.q: What was Manthara's argument that fully convinced Kaikeyi to use the boons?
options: That Bharata would be enslaved and Kaikeyi made a servant once Rama ruled
That Dasharatha planned to exile Bharata
questions.explanation: Manthara warned that under Rama's rule Bharata would be sidelined and Kaikeyi reduced to serving Kaushalya.

questions.q: What did Dasharatha offer Kaikeyi in a desperate attempt to change her mind?
options: Wealth, kingdoms, and to fall at her feet pleading
questions.explanation: Dasharatha begged, offered riches and even prostrated himself, but Kaikeyi remained unmoved.

questions.q: What curse from his past did Dasharatha recall as he lay dying after Rama's departure?
options: The curse of Shravana Kumara's blind parents
questions.explanation: Dasharatha remembered killing the youth Shravana by mistake, whose grieving parents cursed him to die from separation from his own son.

questions.q: How did Bharata respond when he returned and learned of his mother's deed?
options: He condemned Kaikeyi and refused the kingdom
questions.explanation: Bharata was horrified, rebuked Kaikeyi, and refused to accept a throne won by such injustice.

questions.q: Which minister drove the chariot that carried Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana out of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Sumantra, the faithful charioteer and minister, drove the exiles from the city, grieving all the while.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, what was Manthara's earlier relationship to Kaikeyi?
options: A hunchbacked maidservant she had brought from Kekaya
questions.explanation: Manthara was Kaikeyi's loyal hunchbacked maid who had come with her from her father's kingdom of Kekaya.

questions.q: What did Bharata do with Rama's footwear (paduka) after failing to bring him back from the forest?
options: Placed them on the throne and ruled as regent in Rama's name
questions.explanation: Bharata enthroned Rama's sandals and governed Ayodhya as a regent until Rama's return, rejecting kingship for himself.

questions.q: What central virtue does Dasharatha's honoring of the boons exemplify in the Ramayana?
options: Satya and pratijna (truth and keeping one's promise)
questions.explanation: Dasharatha upheld satya, keeping his given word even unto death, a core ideal of dharma in the epic.
